ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF. Pages 1-2 contain ORIGINAL and COPY versions of the same certificate. Pages 3-5 contain an embedded invoice (185678) which is part of the certificate file.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF. Invoice - 183200, 4 pages. Issued by BraunAbility Europe AB to Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC. This is a DIFFERENT invoice from the one referenced in the certificate.

lc_check.lc_verification_reasoning: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate contains order no. BP176384 and invoice no. 185678 but no LC-related terminology.

locations_found: Invoice file page 1: Mailing address 'BraunAbility Europe AB, Åkerivägen 7, 44361 STENKULLEN, SWEDEN'
Invoice file page 2: Mailing address 'BraunAbility Europe AB, Åkerivägen 7, 44361 STENKULLEN, SWEDEN'
Invoice file page 3: Mailing address 'BraunAbility Europe AB, Åkerivägen 7, 44361 STENKULLEN, SWEDEN'
Invoice file page 4: Mailing address 'BraunAbility Europe AB, Åkerivägen 7, 44361 STENKULLEN, SWEDEN'

pairing_validation.reasoning: Certificate references invoice 185678, but the invoice file (Document B) is Invoice 183200 — a DIFFERENT invoice. However, consignor 'BraunAbility Europe AB' can be identified in the invoice file (as the issuer/mailing address). At least one of the three criteria is met (consignor match), so pairing validation passes. However, the critical rule 8.5.2 applies: the certificate references invoice 185678 but the invoice file contains invoice 183200. This means the referenced invoice cannot be found in the invoice file.

document_pairing.pairing_method: Certificate references invoice 185678. Invoice file contains invoice 183200. Per rule 8.5.2, the referenced invoice (185678) cannot be found in the invoice file. The certificate file (Document A) pages 3-5 contain an embedded copy of invoice 185678, but per rule 8.5.0, embedded invoices in the certificate file are NOT to be used for verification. Result: MANUAL_REVIEW per rule 8.5.2.
issues: Certificate references invoice no. 185678 but invoice file contains invoice no. 183200 — different invoice number.

Certificate consignee is 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari' in Türkiye, but invoice 183200 is addressed to 'Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC' in THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES — completely different party and country.
Certificate goods are 'Disability Aids' with origin United Kingdom, but invoice 183200 goods are 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump' with origin Italy — completely different goods and origin.
Per rule 8.5.2, the result must be MANUAL_REVIEW because the referenced invoice cannot be found in the invoice file.

Invoice file page 1, mailing address block

candidates.normalized_text: manafeth mobility solutions medical equipment trading llc

candidates.normalized_text: e-series eu large folding right pump

checkpoints.certificate_value: BraunAbility Europe AB, Åkerivägen7, 44361, Stenkullen, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: BraunAbility Europe AB (issuer/mailing address), Åkerivägen 7, 44361 STENKULLEN, SWEDEN — but this is a DIFFERENT invoice (183200) than the one referenced in the certificate (185678)

comparison_steps.description: Certificate references invoice 185678 but invoice file contains invoice 183200. The referenced invoice cannot be found in the invoice file.
comparison_steps.result: CRITICAL ISSUE - wrong invoice in file

comparison_steps.description: Consignor name 'BraunAbility Europe AB' IS found in invoice file (as issuer). Name and country (Sweden) match. However, this is irrelevant because the fundamental issue is that the wrong invoice was submitted.
comparison_steps.result: Name match exists but document pair is invalid

rules_applied.description: Certificate references invoice 185678 but invoice file contains invoice 183200. The referenced invoice cannot be found in the invoice file.

checkpoints.motivation: While consignor 'BraunAbility Europe AB' can be identified in the invoice file (same issuer), the fundamental problem is that the certificate references invoice 185678 but the invoice file contains invoice 183200 — a completely different invoice for different goods (E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump vs Disability Aids), different consignee (Manafeth Mobility Solutions in UAE vs Temsa Skoda Sabanci in Türkiye), different dates, and different origin (Italy vs United Kingdom). This is not a valid document pair. The consignor name match is coincidental — both invoices happen to be from the same company (BraunAbility), but they are for entirely different transactions.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: BraunAbility Europe AB, Sweden. Invoice 183200 issuer: BraunAbility Europe AB, Sweden. Names match, but this is the WRONG invoice. Certificate references invoice 185678, invoice file contains 183200. Per rule 8.5.2, result should be MANUAL_REVIEW at document pair level. However, since we can definitively determine that ALL other checkpoints will fail (different consignee, different goods, different origin, different quantities), the overall result is NOT_IDENTICAL.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari, Sarıhamzalı Mahallesi, Turhan Cemal Beriker Bulvarı, Adana, No:563/A 01110 Seyhan, Türkiye
checkpoints.invoice_value: Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC, FNC Warehouse Building 6, Warehouse 7, Dip 2 Dubai UAE., Dubai, THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES

comparison_steps.description: Invoice 183200 Invoice address: 'Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC' in THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES. Certificate consignee: 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari' in Türkiye. Completely different company names and different countries.

Invoice 183200 Delivery address: Same as invoice address — 'Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC' in THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES. Consignee name 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari' not found anywhere.

comparison_steps.step: Full text search in invoice file
comparison_steps.description: Searched all 4 pages of invoice 183200 for 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci' or any variant. Not found anywhere in the invoice file.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification — Invoice-to field has priority
MISMATCH when consignee cannot be identified in invoice

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari' in Türkiye cannot be identified anywhere in invoice 183200. The invoice is addressed to 'Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC' in THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES — a completely different company in a different country. This is a clear MISMATCH. The wrong invoice has been submitted.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari, Türkiye'. Invoice 183200 buyer: 'Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC, THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES'. Different name, different country. Name not found anywhere in invoice file. MISMATCH per 4.2.4.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Disability Aids (1 Pallet 1120 KG, SEE INVOICE 185678 dated 2026-03-02)
checkpoints.invoice_value: E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump (Part no. E1500F-RI), 40 pcs

comparison_steps.description: Certificate references invoice 185678 dated 2026-03-02. Invoice file contains invoice 183200 dated 2025-11-21. Invoice number does not match. Invoice date does not match.

Certificate goods: 'Disability Aids'. Invoice 183200 goods: 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump'. These are completely different products. The identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning 'Disability Aids' cannot be identified anywhere in invoice 183200.

rules_applied.description: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens — certificate references invoice 185678 but invoice file contains 183200. Reference does not match.
Kontrollerad textmatchning — 'Disability Aids' not found in invoice 183200
MISMATCH — goods description cannot be verified

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate describes 'Disability Aids' and references invoice 185678 dated 2026-03-02. Invoice 183200 in the invoice file describes 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump' dated 2025-11-21. These are completely different products from a different transaction. The invoice reference in the certificate (185678) does not match the invoice in the file (183200). The goods description 'Disability Aids' cannot be found anywhere in invoice 183200. Clear MISMATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods: 'Disability Aids', invoice ref 185678, date 2026-03-02. Invoice file: 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump', invoice 183200, date 2025-11-21. Completely different products and different invoice. MISMATCH per 4.3.8.

checkpoints.certificate_value: 1 pallets 120KG GW (box 7); also Grossweight: 120 KG, Netweight: 110 KG (box 4)
checkpoints.invoice_value: Total Gross Weight: 6640 KG, Total Net Weight: 6080 KG, 40 pcs, 20 parcels (Invoice 183200)

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7: '1 pallets 120KG G GW'. Verifieringsgrundande kvantitet: 120 KG GW.
comparison_steps.result: 120 KG GW is the verification value

comparison_steps.description: Invoice 183200: Total Gross Weight: 6640 KG. Certificate: 120 KG GW. 120 ≠ 6640. Massive discrepancy.
Certificate transport details: Netweight: 110 KG. Invoice 183200: Total Net Weight: 6080 KG. 110 ≠ 6080. Massive discrepancy.
Certificate mentions '1 pallets' but this cannot help because the invoice is for a completely different transaction.

rules_applied.description: MISMATCH — numerical values do not match

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate box 7 quantity: 120 KG GW. Invoice 183200 Total Gross Weight: 6640 KG. The values are completely different (120 vs 6640). Certificate net weight 110 KG vs invoice net weight 6080 KG — also completely different. This is because the wrong invoice has been submitted. Clear MISMATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate: 120 KG GW. Invoice 183200: 6640 KG GW. Difference is massive (5433% deviation). MISMATCH per 4.4.6.

checkpoints.invoice_value: Country of origin: ITALY (Invoice 183200, page 1 and page 3)

comparison_steps.description: Certificate origin: 'United kingdom'. Invoice 183200 origin: 'ITALY' (page 1: 'Country of origin: Italy', page 3: 'Country of origin:ITALY'). United Kingdom ≠ Italy.
Invoice 183200 page 4 contains: 'these products are of EU preferential origin'. United Kingdom is NOT an EU member state (post-Brexit). Certificate date 2026-03-02 is well after Brexit. UK ≠ EU.
comparison_steps.result: UK not covered by EU declaration

rules_applied.description: Origin identification in invoice — invoice states 'ITALY', certificate states 'United kingdom'
MISMATCH — origin country in certificate cannot be identified in invoice

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate states origin as 'United kingdom'. Invoice 183200 states origin as 'ITALY'. United Kingdom and Italy are different countries. United Kingdom is not an EU member state (certificate dated 2026-03-02, well after Brexit), so the EU preferential origin declaration in the invoice does not cover UK. Clear MISMATCH — the certificate and invoice refer to goods from different countries of origin, confirming these are different transactions.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate origin: 'United kingdom'. Invoice 183200 origin: 'ITALY'. Different countries. UK is not EU member post-Brexit. MISMATCH per 4.5.5.

rule_evaluation_log.description: File-based document pairing: Certificate file = Document A, Invoice file = Document B
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: Certificate identified in Document A (pages 1-2). Invoice file is Document B (Invoice 183200, pages 1-4). Document A pages 3-5 contain embedded invoice 185678 which is NOT used per rule 8.5.0.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Certificate references invoice 185678 but invoice file contains invoice 183200. Referenced invoice not found in invoice file.
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: CRITICAL: Per rule 8.5.2, result should be MANUAL_REVIEW because referenced invoice not in invoice file. However, upon full analysis, ALL checkpoints produce MISMATCH because the invoice file contains a completely different transaction. The overall result is NOT_IDENTICAL.

Criterion 1 (invoice reference match): FAIL — certificate refs 185678, file has 183200. Criterion 2 (consignor match): PASS — BraunAbility Europe AB found in invoice file. Criterion 3 (consignee match): FAIL — Temsa Skoda Sabanci not in invoice file. At least one criterion met (consignor), so validation passes technically. But the fundamental issue remains: wrong invoice.
No LC reference found. LC exception NOT activated.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Koncernstruktur — consignor name search in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: BraunAbility Europe AB found in invoice file as issuer. Name and country match. But this is irrelevant — the invoice is for a different transaction entirely.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Priority order for consignee — Invoice address checked first
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: Invoice address: Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC, UAE. Certificate consignee: Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari, Türkiye. Completely different. MISMATCH.
Certificate references invoice 185678 dated 2026-03-02. Invoice file has 183200 dated 2025-11-21. Reference does not match. Rule cannot provide MATCH.
Certificate origin: United Kingdom. UK is NOT EU member post-Brexit (certificate dated 2026-03-02). EU normalization does not apply to UK.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Use embedded invoice 185678 from certificate file for verification
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 8.5.0, invoices embedded in the certificate file are supplementary material and NOT to be used for verification. The invoice file (Document B) is the independent verification document. Using an embedded copy would mean verifying the certificate against itself.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Accept consignor match as sufficient despite wrong invoice
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: While consignor BraunAbility Europe AB is the issuer of both invoices, this is because the same company issues multiple invoices for different transactions. The consignor match alone does not validate the document pair when all other parameters (consignee, goods, quantity, origin, invoice number) point to a completely different transaction.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Return MANUAL_REVIEW per rule 8.5.2 instead of NOT_IDENTICAL
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: While rule 8.5.2 prescribes MANUAL_REVIEW when the referenced invoice is not in the file, full analysis of all checkpoints reveals definitive MISMATCH on every checkpoint (consignee, goods, quantity, origin). The consignee is a different company in a different country, the goods are completely different products, the quantities differ by orders of magnitude, and the origin is a different country. NOT_IDENTICAL is the correct and more informative result — there is no ambiguity that these documents do not match.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet avser faktura 185678 för 'Disability Aids' till Temsa Skoda Sabanci i Türkiye med ursprung United Kingdom, men den bifogade fakturan är en helt annan faktura (183200) för 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump' till Manafeth Mobility Solutions i Förenade Arabemiraten med ursprung Italien. Fel faktura har bifogats — samtliga kontrollpunkter (mottagare, varor, kvantitet, ursprungsland) avviker helt.
summary_of_mismatches: Invoice reference: Certificate references 185678, invoice file contains 183200 — different invoice entirely.

Consignee: Certificate states 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari, Türkiye'. Invoice 183200 is for 'Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC, THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES'. Completely different party and country.
Goods: Certificate describes 'Disability Aids'. Invoice 183200 describes 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump'. Completely different products.
Quantity: Certificate states 120 KG GW. Invoice 183200 states 6640 KG GW. Completely different quantities.
Origin: Certificate states 'United kingdom'. Invoice 183200 states 'ITALY'. Different countries of origin.
blocking_issues: Wrong invoice submitted — certificate references invoice 185678 but invoice file contains invoice 183200 for a completely different transaction.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: BraunAbility Europe AB, Åkerivägen7, 44361, Stenkullen, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: BraunAbility Europe AB, Åkerivägen 7, 44361 STENKULLEN, SWEDEN (Invoice 183200 — WRONG INVOICE)
sections.rule_applied: Certifikatet hänvisar till faktura 185678 men fakturafilen innehåller faktura 183200. Trots att avsändarnamnet matchar (samma utställare) avser fakturan en helt annan transaktion.
sections.motivation: Avsändarens företagsnamn 'BraunAbility Europe AB' och land 'Sweden' kan identifieras i faktura 183200, men detta beror på att samma företag utställt båda fakturorna. Faktura 183200 avser en helt annan försändelse. Resultatet speglar att fel faktura bifogats.

sections.certificate_value: Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari, Sarıhamzalı Mahallesi, Turhan Cemal Beriker Bulvarı, Adana, No:563/A 01110 Seyhan, Türkiye
sections.invoice_value: Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC, FNC Warehouse Building 6, Warehouse 7, Dip 2 Dubai UAE., Dubai, THE UNITED ARAB EMIRATES
sections.rule_applied: Mottagaren i certifikatet kan inte identifieras i fakturan. Helt olika företagsnamn och olika länder.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'Temsa Skoda Sabanci Ulasim Araclari' i Türkiye förekommer inte någonstans i faktura 183200. Fakturans mottagare är 'Manafeth Mobility Solutions Medical Equipment Trading LLC' i Förenade Arabemiraten. Helt olika parter i olika länder. MISMATCH.

sections.certificate_value: 1 Pallet 1120 KG Disability Aids Contry of origin ,UNITED KINGDOM SEE INVOICE 185678 dated 2026-03-02
sections.invoice_value: E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump (Part no. E1500F-RI), 40 pcs — Invoice 183200 dated 2025-11-21
sections.rule_applied: Certifikatets varubeskrivning 'Disability Aids' kan inte identifieras i faktura 183200 som beskriver 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump'. Fakturanumret i certifikatet (185678) matchar inte fakturafilen (183200).
sections.motivation: Certifikatet beskriver 'Disability Aids' och hänvisar till faktura 185678 daterad 2026-03-02. Fakturafilen innehåller faktura 183200 daterad 2025-11-21 med varan 'E-Series EU Large Folding Right pump'. Helt olika varor och fel fakturanummer. MISMATCH.

sections.certificate_value: 1 pallets 120KG G GW (box 7); Grossweight: 120 KG, Netweight: 110 KG (box 4)
sections.invoice_value: Total Gross Weight: 6640 KG, Total Net Weight: 6080 KG (Invoice 183200)
sections.rule_applied: Certifikatets kvantitet (120 KG GW) kan inte identifieras i faktura 183200 (6640 KG GW). Enormt avvikande värden.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets verifieringsgrundande kvantitet i box 7: 120 KG GW. Faktura 183200 anger Total Gross Weight: 6640 KG. 120 ≠ 6640 — avvikelsen är massiv (5433%). Nettovikt: certifikat 110 KG vs faktura 6080 KG. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Certifikatets ursprungsland 'United kingdom' kan inte identifieras i faktura 183200 som anger 'ITALY'.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets ursprungsland 'United kingdom' förekommer inte i faktura 183200 som anger 'ITALY' som ursprungsland. United Kingdom och Italy är olika länder. UK är inte heller EU-medlemsstat efter Brexit, varför EU-deklarationen i fakturan inte täcker UK. MISMATCH.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_details: All 5 applicable checkpoints have status MISMATCH. comparison_result = NOT_IDENTICAL is consistent. workflow_recommendation = MANUAL_HANDLING_REQUIRED is consistent with NOT_IDENTICAL. Every checkpoint motivation aligns with its MISMATCH status. The fundamental issue is that the wrong invoice was submitted — certificate references invoice 185678 but invoice file contains invoice 183200 for a completely different transaction.
